text: On Cisco NX-OS, when a BGP neighbor shows Idle(Admin) or Idle state with tcp_connect_failed, verify underlay reachability first. Use 'ping <peer_ip> source-interface <loopback>' to test reachability from the correct interface. Check 'show ip route <peer_ip>' to confirm the route exists. Verify the BGP configuration with 'show bgp neighbors <ip>': confirm remote-as, update-source, and local-as match expectations. On NX-OS, BGP uses the loopback0 address by default — ensure update-source loopback0 is configured if the peer expects loopback-sourced sessions. Check 'show bgp neighbors <ip> | inc State' for FSM state and error codes. Common causes: ACL blocking TCP 179, wrong update-source, remote-as mismatch, or peer not configured on the remote side.

On Cisco NX-OS, perform a BGP soft reset with 'clear bgp ipv4 unicast <peer_ip> soft' for a bidirectional soft reset, or 'clear bgp ipv4 unicast <peer_ip> soft in' for inbound only. The soft reset sends a route-refresh message to the peer requesting re-advertisement without tearing the session. Use soft resets when: route policy changes need to take effect, prefix counts are lower than expected without a session flap, or you need to refresh the RIB without traffic impact. For outbound policy changes use 'clear bgp ipv4 unicast <peer_ip> soft out'. Avoid 'clear bgp ipv4 unicast <peer_ip>' (hard reset) during production — it drops the TCP session and triggers full reconvergence. Always check impact with 'show bgp neighbors <ip> | inc prefixes' before and after.

On Cisco NX-OS, when a BGP session is Established but shows 0 prefixes received, check the following. First, verify 'show bgp neighbors <ip> | inc prefixes' — if Prefixes received is 0 but session is Established, the issue is policy or advertisement. On the sender, use 'show bgp neighbors <ip> advertised-routes' to see what is being sent. If the sender is advertising but the receiver sees nothing, check inbound route-maps on the receiver with 'show bgp neighbors <ip> | inc route-map'. Also verify address-family activation: the neighbor must be activated under 'address-family ipv4 unicast' with 'activate' keyword. On NX-OS, address-family activation is not automatic — an unconfigured address-family results in zero prefixes even with a live session.

section_title: Route Reflector Failure on NX-OS Fabrics
text: On Cisco NX-OS spine-leaf fabrics using route reflectors, a route reflector failure causes all iBGP client sessions to fail simultaneously. When multiple leaf switches show BGP neighbors in Idle or Active state toward the same route reflector IP, diagnose the route reflector first. Use 'show bgp summary' on the RR — if the BGP process has restarted, session reconvergence happens automatically as clients reconnect. Check 'show bgp neighbors' on the RR to see which clients are connected. On NX-OS, 'show bgp event-history errors' and 'show bgp event-history notifications' show recent error events. Do not reset individual leaf sessions until the RR is confirmed stable — premature resets increase reconvergence time. If the RR is reachable but sessions are stuck in Active, check if the RR BGP process is running: 'show bgp process' should show running state.

Essential Cisco NX-OS BGP verification commands: 'show bgp summary' shows all neighbors, state, uptime, prefix counts. 'show bgp neighbors <ip>' shows full session detail including FSM state, timers, capabilities. 'show bgp neighbors <ip> advertised-routes' shows routes being advertised to the peer. 'show bgp neighbors <ip> received-routes' shows all received routes. 'show bgp ipv4 unicast <prefix>' shows RIB entry and path selection. 'show ip route bgp' shows installed BGP routes. 'show bgp process' verifies the BGP process is running. 'show bgp event-history errors' shows recent error events. For EVPN: 'show bgp l2vpn evpn summary'. For route reflector: 'show bgp neighbors <client_ip> | inc cluster'.
